spans.text: the solitude of the vast deep
The gallant vessel scuds before the wind
thy whirlwinds roar Over seas of solitary amplitude
his high-foundered bark Where no dim watch-light gleams
He dreamt not of strange lands
Beyond the rolling of the watery waste
In some rude shaggy spot , by fortune placed , He dreamt not of strange lands , and empires spread , Beyond the rolling of the watery waste
In some rude shaggy spot , by fortune placed , He dreamt not of strange lands , and empires spread , Beyond the rolling of the watery waste
Gods of the storm the dreary space might sweep , And shapes of death , and gliding spectres gaunt ,
hummed the shores with early industry
locks , The houseless savage slept beneath the foam-beat rocks
upon the dreamy verge Of instinct
the track of his dominion wide
While sounds of fervent industry arise , A thousand pennants float
thy voice , upon the rock she stood Gigantic , the rude scene she marked — she cried , Let there be intercourse , and the great flood Waft the rich plenty to these shores denied
Queen of the waters ! who didst open the gate Of Commerce , and display in lands
their massy piles thy cothons rear
the deep a solemn shadow cast
The sound of mighty generations past
thy kingly merchant's ' thronged resort , And gold and purple gleam
thy glittering galleys sweep along — The steady rowers to the strokes incline , And chant in unison their choral song
The fine-wrought sails , which looms of Egypt
The fine-wrought sails , which looms of Egypt
thy seat Amid the sea , thy beauty and thy fame The deep , that rolls low-murmuring at thy feet
To thee their flocks Arabia's princes
Queen of the waters ! who is like to thee , Replenished in thy might , and throned on the sea
The ships of Tarshish of thy glory
Surveying the dim earth and seas below
the dim earth and seas below
( Amid the brazen cymbal's hollow ring ) And aged priests the solemn feast prepare
To her their nightly orisons they sing ; That she may look from her high throne , and guide The wandering bark secure along the trackless tide
The wandering bark secure along the trackless tide
great Carmel's woody height , Where nightly fires to grisly Baal burn
of the sweet light of summer
more vast amid the rushing storm
the bark along the placid bay Of Tamiatis keeps her easy-winding way
we safe from scenes of peril past , No danger lurks in this serene retreat
safe from scenes of peril past
